title: abies engelmannii
intro: Abies engelmannii, also known as Engelmann spruce, is a species of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The bark of this plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including sesquiterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The essential oil extracted from the bark of Abies engelmannii has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Abies engelmannii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
